column8: Low-cost clamp to ~3.6V for small transients. Good when you specifically want ~3.6V clamp. Check power rating (250–500 mW) and leakage.

Notes: Use with Rseries to clamp midpoint to 3.3V rail. Preferred when you already have a solid 3.3V rail. Use BAT54 for small currents, SS14 for higher current.

column7: Place between BAT_ADC node and MCU pin (or between BAT_ADC node and clamp diode) to limit clamp current. Use value 1k–10k depending on divider impedance and ADC sample cap.

Description: Unidirectional TVS diode for transient suppression (protects from spikes)
Notes: For large transients/clamps: choose TVS rated for working standoff and expected surge. Note: TVS clamp voltage is usually higher than zener knee; verify clamp vs battery level.

column7: Small cap from BAT_ADC to GND to stabilize ADC sampling with high-value divider. 10–100 nF recommended.

Notes: Documented MOSFET for heater: RLB8721PBF (TO-220). IMPORTANT: verify Rds(on) at VGS=3.3V in the datasheet; board currently drives gate from MCU (3.3V). If Rds(on) is high at 3.3V, use a logic-level MOSFET or add a gate driver.

column7: Search for low Rds(on) SMD parts with explicit low-Vgs characterization. Good for compact boards with thermal vias.

column7: Use TO-220 for easy heatsink mounting and prototyping. Verify mechanical clearance.
